title: Model, Surface, Capability, and Constraint Selection · Mastery Check
summary: Choose between Claude and Codex surfaces for a repository and document task. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a capability and surface decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.
Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.
Match reasoning effort and autonomy to task complexity, reversibility, and verification cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Surface Fit evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Surface Fit assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use a changed quiz case for prompt model surface that tests this competency: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Surface Fit has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Surface Fit precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: All model surfaces differ only in visual appearance.

Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the quiz Surface Fit case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
